# Task Generation Readiness Checklist: Tax Document Persistence Foundation

**Purpose**: Validate whether SPEC 003 requirements are complete, clear,
consistent, and measurable enough for later task generation without inferring
out-of-scope behavior.
**Created**: 2026-07-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command
based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all future task-generating requirements anchored to explicit
  SPEC 003 requirements, success criteria, contracts, or plan sections rather
  than implied implementation intent? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001-FR-023,
  Spec §SC-001-SC-011, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Are all allowed implementation surfaces documented with enough
  specificity to prevent task generation outside the persistence adapter,
  migration, configuration, domain restore, and test locations? [Completeness,
  Spec §AR-001, Spec §AR-010, Plan §Project Structure, Quickstart
  §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK003 Are persistence dependency and configuration requirements bounded
  to persistence support and adapter testing without implying domain or
  application dependencies on persistence frameworks? [Completeness,
  Spec §FR-016, Spec §AR-011, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK004 Are durable migration documentation updates specified with the
  required target tables, columns, constraints, temporal mappings, and PFV
  references before tasks are generated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015,
  Contract §Migration Documentation Updates]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the domain restore requirement narrow enough for task
  generation to add only framework-free rehydration behavior and not new
  document-specific issuance logic? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007,
  Plan §Structure Decision, Data Model §Persistence Entity Mapping]
- [x] CHK006 Are repository `save` semantics clear for create, same-aggregate
  update, duplicate `accessKey`, duplicate issuance identity, and missing
  lookup behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Contract §TaxDocumentRepository]
- [x] CHK007 Are sequence reservation requirements clear enough to distinguish
  requested-value reservation, exact repeated idempotency, conflicting
  duplicate failure, and deferred auto-numbering policy? [Clarity, Spec
  §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Spec §PFV-PER-005, Contract §SequenceNumberPort]
- [x] CHK008 Are application-facing persistence error categories defined with
  stable names and forbidden framework exception leakage so tasks do not
  expose database-specific types inward? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §AR-006,
  Contract §Persistence Error Translation]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are scope exclusions consistent across the spec, plan, quickstart,
  and contracts for REST, SRI, XML generation/signing, storage, queue, webhook,
  bootstrap runtime behavior, and document-specific issuance flows?
  [Consistency, Spec §Scope Boundaries, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Summary,
  Quickstart §Prerequisites]
- [x] CHK010 Are PFV statuses consistent across the spec, plan, research, data
  model, contracts, and quickstart so tasks do not accidentally resolve
  deferred behavior? [Consistency, Spec §Pending Functional Validations,
  Plan §Pending Functional Validations, Research §PFV Traceability Summary,
  Data Model §Deferred Data and Behavior, Quickstart §Deferred PFV Summary]
- [x] CHK011 Are schema relationship and constraint requirements consistent
  between the plan, data model, schema contract, and migration documentation
  contract? [Consistency, Plan §Schema Relationship and Constraint Design,
  Data Model §Target Tables, Contract §Persistence Schema]
- [x] CHK012 Are test requirements consistent with the constitution's layered
  testing rules, keeping domain/application tests infrastructure-free while
  allowing persistence adapter tests to use approved infrastructure support?
  [Consistency, Constitution §Layered Testing Requirements, Spec §AR-002,
  Plan §Testing]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can each success criterion be objectively evaluated by future
  tasks without requiring REST, SRI, XML storage, queue, webhook, or
  document-specific artifacts? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-011,
  Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK014 Are persistence preservation requirements measurable for all
  required fields, including access key, issuance identity, issue date,
  document state, authorization state, authorization number, and authorized
  timestamp? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-011]
- [x] CHK015 Are temporal precision requirements measurable enough for future
  tasks to choose assertions without inventing timezone or timestamp semantics?
  [Measurability, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Temporal Rules, Data Model §Temporal
  Rules]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK016 Are primary task-worthy scenarios separated for persist/load,
  duplicate protection, sequence reservation, and architecture/migration
  traceability? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec
  §User Story 3, Spec §User Story 4]
- [x] CHK017 Are alternate scenarios for missing repository results and exact
  repeated sequence reservation documented clearly enough for task generation?
  [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §SC-010, Contract
  §TaxDocumentRepository, Contract §SequenceNumberPort]
- [x] CHK018 Are exception scenarios for invalid persisted state, unknown
  canonical enum values, inconsistent relationships, duplicate races, and
  generic persistence failures covered in requirements or contracts?
  [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §Persistence Error Translation]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are migration failure, rollback, or repair requirements either
  explicitly defined for task generation or intentionally deferred outside this
  feature? [Edge Case, Spec §PFV-PER-006, Research §Defer Migration Rollback
  and Repair Workflows, Plan §Pending Functional Validations]
- [x] CHK020 Are archive, purge, delete, update, and production data correction
  behaviors explicitly excluded or deferred so tasks do not invent lifecycle
  behavior beyond restrictive relationships? [Edge Case, Plan §Schema
  Relationship and Constraint Design, Spec §PFV-PER-007, Contract §Delete and
  Update Restrictions]
- [x] CHK021 Are Spanish legacy naming exceptions constrained to migration
  mapping documentation and excluded from target schema, persistence entities,
  domain, application, and tests? [Edge Case, Spec §NR-002, Constitution
  §English Canonical Terminology]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK022 Are concurrency and reliability requirements strong enough to
  require database uniqueness and transactional behavior rather than
  application-only duplicate checks? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-012, Plan
  §Performance Goals, Research §Enforce Uniqueness]
- [x] CHK023 Are sensitive diagnostic and error message exclusions specified
  for persistence failures and audit-related logging without introducing audit
  table implementation tasks? [Non-Functional, Spec §Sensitive Data
  Exclusions, Contract §Persistence Error Translation]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK024 Are assumptions about feature `002-tax-document-issuance-foundation`
  and its existing application port contracts explicit enough to prevent
  unnecessary port redesign tasks? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan
  §Layer and Boundary Design, Spec §AR-012, Contract §Persistence Port
  Implementations]
- [x] CHK025 Are deferred dependencies on legacy compatibility, XML storage,
  audit persistence, production data migration, and auto-numbering policy
  documented with PFV IDs and excluded from task generation? [Dependency,
  Spec §PFV-PER-002, Spec §PFV-PER-003, Spec §PFV-PER-004, Spec
  §PFV-PER-005, Spec §PFV-PER-008]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK026 Are any remaining ambiguous schema names, relationship semantics,
  constraint semantics, or persistence behavior required to become Pending
  Naming Decisions or Pending Functional Validations before task generation?
  [Ambiguity, Spec §NR-006, Spec §FR-023]
- [x] CHK027 Are persistence configuration allowances clearly separated from
  forbidden bootstrap runtime behavior so future tasks do not create a
  bootstrap package? [Conflict, Spec §AR-001, Spec §AR-010, Plan §Project
  Structure, Quickstart §Verify Forbidden Scope]

## Notes

- Items are requirement-quality checks, not implementation verification steps.
- Check items off as completed: `[x]`.
- All checklist items are satisfied by explicit, traceable SPEC 003
  documentation as of 2026-07-06.
- No unresolved readiness findings remain before task generation.
